topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: RSP QB1 with DoT 85.6 and ADP 11.39 — sits right at the 1.11 turn. With only 3 QBs rostered and a 1-year contract expiring, Mendoza is the cleanest BPA + need overlap available.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB3 (DoT 84.6) and only 3 RBs on roster. Goes off-board at 1.11 vs ADP but medium RSP affinity gives Waldman's 'underrated talent' tag real weight here.

topTargets.name: Skyler Bell
topTargets.reasoning: RSP WR4 with Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp at DoT 85.9. Doesn't appear on MFL ADP top 30, meaning he'll be available — classic blended-board reach for a Starter-grade WR.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Industry favorite at ADP 12.71 — very likely on the board at 1.11. Adds to an already deep WR room (7) but consensus value is hard to pass on with $5.1M cap and only 1 pick.

topTargets.name: Demond Claiborne
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB4 (DoT 84.5) with Shady McCoy traits. Falls past 1.11 on most boards but addresses RB-3 depth and fits taxi-eligible pricing if cap forces a low-base contract.

capPosture: Tight at $39.9M used / $5.1M space — a single round-1 rookie fits on the active roster but a $1M+ base salary likely forces taxi placement to preserve flexibility.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Already deep at TE (5) so logic says pass, but RSP DoT 84.4 with classic move-TE profile and a former QB pedigree is the kind of value a medium-RSP-affinity GM with one pick might gamble on if the QB/RB tier breaks badly.

summary: DMOC enters with a single bullet at 1.11 and a cap-tight roster that's WR/TE-heavy and thin at QB and RB. The most likely play is Fernando Mendoza if he slides — addressing the expiring contract and 3-deep QB room — with Emmett Johnson or Demond Claiborne as the RB pivot if a contender jumps Mendoza. Expect taxi placement to manage the $5.1M cap squeeze.
